title: Codex review verdict
description: The machine-readable verdict Codex emits as its final message each review round (via --output-schema, written by -o/--output-last-message). IMPORTANT: this schema is consumed by `codex exec --output-schema`, whose backend (OpenAI structured outputs, STRICT mode) FORBIDS allOf/if-then/oneOf and requires EVERY property in `required`, with optionals expressed as nullable. So this schema constrains SHAPE ONLY. The semantic invariants (APPROVE<=>no blockers, REVISE>=1 blocker, REJECT justified) are enforced by the orchestrator's consistent(verdict) check (DESIGN §4/§7) — that is the only enforcement; the schema cannot express it. There is no separate prose channel: the human-readable review is review_markdown (stdout under --output-schema/--json is JSON, not prose).

decision.description: APPROVE = converged (consistent() also requires blocking_issues empty). REVISE = iterate (consistent() requires >=1 blocking issue). REJECT = fundamental flaw, escalate (consistent() requires >=1 blocking issue OR a reject_reason).

confidence.description: 0..1, the reviewer's confidence. (min/max omitted: OpenAI strict mode forbids numeric bounds.) The orchestrator treats an out-of-range value as a consistent() FAILURE (re-prompt once) — NOT a silent clamp, since e.g. 85 would clamp to 1.0 and sail through the gate. Load-bearing: an APPROVE below cfg.min_confidence[stage] downgrades to awaiting_human (DESIGN §7).

review_markdown.description: Full human-readable review, Markdown. The orchestrator renders reviews/<stage>-<round>-review.md from this. The prose channel — do NOT expect prose on stdout.

blocking_issues.description: Issues that MUST be fixed before approval. The orchestrator requires this empty for APPROVE and non-empty for REVISE (the schema can't enforce that in strict mode).

id.description: Short id, e.g. B1/BI-001. Ids are emitted fresh by an independent reviewer each round and are NOT stable; never key oscillation logic on them (DESIGN §10).

location.description: Where in the artifact (section / file:line). Used with title for the orchestrator's stable content key.

suggested_fix.description: A PROPOSAL, not a command; the author may deviate (noting why) if unsafe or out of scope.

non_blocking_suggestions.description: Nits that do not block approval and never add a round. [] if none.

reject_reason.description: Required (non-null) for REJECT unless the flaw is itemized in blocking_issues (orchestrator-enforced). null otherwise.

addressed_previous.description: IDs of prior-round blocking issues the reviewer judges resolved this round; [] if none. Advisory: validated by the orchestrator against the actual prior verdict's ids before being trusted.

regressions.description: Resolved-ledger items (DESIGN §3) the reviewer finds REGRESSED this round — previously-fixed blockers now broken again. [] if none. The orchestrator forbids APPROVE when this is non-empty (consistent()). The ledger of 'must stay fixed' keys is supplied to the reviewer in its prompt.

key.description: The resolved-ledger content key that regressed.

evidence.description: The specific diff/excerpt showing the regression (E2) — not just an assertion. The orchestrator validates this against the artifact delta; an unverifiable claim is downgraded to a normal finding, NOT a hard APPROVE-block.

dispute_rulings.description: For each author dispute carried into this review (DESIGN §5): the reviewer's ruling. [] if no open disputes. A 'conceded' ruling moves the item to STATE.accepted_deviations — it no longer blocks APPROVE and is excluded from oscillation scoring (E3). Persistent 'upheld' across rounds escalates to the human.

ref.description: The disputed item's id / content key.
